channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Sony has not decided on a price or when to launch the PS6
item.content:encoded:
Sony have put to bed any rumours of a forthcoming PlayStation 6 launch following a question by a journalist in their latest earnings call.

“We have not yet decided on at what timing we will launch the new console, or at what prices,” said Sony president and CEO Hiroki Totoki via a translator.

The current high cost of RAM and components is the factor here. “Looking at the current circumstances, the memory price is also expected to be very high FY 2027, because there will still be a shortage of supply. So under that assumption, we must think carefully what we will do,” he said. “We would like to think about various simulations, including changing business models to come up with the best solution and strategy.”

He also stated that there would be no further increases to the PlayStation 5 as for the rest of the calendar year 2026 Sony has acquired the necessary volume of materials it needs and has “to a certain extent agreed on the price itself”.

“However, ” he concludes, “I can confirm the PS6 will be the size of a large dog and will be shaped like an unfurling  spring Tulip.”

Fine, I made that up,  he didn’t say that –  but it probably will be.

There have been almost daily rumours about the PlayStation 6,  the latest ‘leaked‘ specs are: Custom AMD Zen 6 architecture, RDNA 5 graphics, dedicated “Neural Arrays” for built-in AI upscaling (PSSR 2.0). and 32GB of DDR7 RAM. Given that 32GB of DDR5 RAM currently costs over £400 you can see why Sony is holding off selling a new console.

The PS6 is said to have the codename of Orion, but there is also a second console, Project Canis, which is a handheld device built around Zen 6c CPU cores, RDNA 5 compute units, 16GB of LPDDR5X memory. This device is said to be backwards compatible with PS4 and PS5 games.

As it appears the PS6 is now not going to be out until 2027 or 2028 then even if all those leaked specs are true, they are probably going to change.

For a little fun, and as by the time the PS6 is out we will be ruled by our AI overlords, I asked them what they thought the PS6 would look like. I wasn’t far off with my unfurling tulip joke.

Source: VGC



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/08/sony-ps6-launch-date-price/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Leon Must Die Forever free DLC minigame out now for Resident Evil Requiem
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has released Leon Must Die Forever, a free DLC minigame that’s been added to Resident Evil Requiem today, across all launch platforms.

Similar to Ethan Must Die for Resident Evil 7, Leon Must Die Forever is a bonus game mode that’s unlocked after completing the main story, putting you back into the shoes of Leon Kennedy and tasking you with battling back through previous parts of the game with stronger enemy variants, increasingly difficult ranks, and racing to beat the final boss against the clock.

As you play, you’ll be boosted by defeating enemies to fill an enhancement gauge, unlocking “enhancer abiltiies” that are exclusive to this game mode and will differ from run to run.

Alongside the new game mode, Capcom says the update also does this:

  • Various bug fixes across platforms have been addressed
  • Added PC support for the DualSense® wireless controller’s adaptive triggers, haptic feedback, and motion sensor features

Resident Evil Requiem has been the fastest-selling game in the franchise, already racing to more than 6 million copies sold since release at the end of February. With such early success, Capcom is at work on story DLC, but released this as a little freebie to keep the game active.

And what a game it was, garnering no small amount of praise, including a 9/10 from us.

“Resident Evil Requiem is a superb entry in the series and feels like a ‘best of’ at times with the persistent Easter eggs, references, and gameplay callbacks to the full range of earlier games,” said Steve, “There is a vast amount of wider lore for fans of that part of Capcom’s legendary titles, but even newcomers will be caught up in the narrative and balls to the wall gameplay. RE Requiem is up there with the very best of the series.”

You can also read our thoughts in the very impressive Switch 2 version of the game.

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/08/leon-must-die-forever-free-dlc-minigame-out-now-for-resident-evil-requiem/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: What We Played – TerraTech Legion, Saros & Mixtape
item.content:encoded:
I always get a little bit thrown off by the short week after a bank holiday, so we’re up to Friday again, and another sunny weekend ahead – I’ve no idea, really, I’m just assuming. But what has been played in the past few days? Well, I’ve given TerraTech Legion a little go, and it’s rather good after my first couple runs. I’ve also got a few other things bubbling away that you’ll hear about soon, and so we’ll quickly jump across to the rest of the team.

Jason has, over the last few weeks, played way more TerraTech than I have, and he’s not quite done with it yet as there’s just a few unlocks that he’s trying to snag. Alongsdie that, he’s been enjoying some more Final Fantasy VII Remake Intergrade, taking it slowly, as well as “one game I can’t talk about”.

Jim’s put Hitman down and picked up Silent Hill 2 – “it’s a slow burn though I assume that’s a deliberate attempt at being faithful to the original game.” He’s also had a quick jolly with Thank Goodness You’re Here, “which is one of the funniest games I’ve played in a long time, layered with plenty of absurd English humour.

Thank Goodness Youre Here

Getting to play Shadow of the Colossus for the very first time is Reuben, who’s already convinced that it’s “phenomenal”. He’s been mixing that with Dragon Quest I: HD-2D Remaster as a post-work wind-down kind of game.

Aran has been playing Motorslice for review, which will have a full review for next week, while Gamoc has been spookying it up with a Ghost Master Resurrected review, and continuing to grapple with Starfield, despite a save file bug regularly interrupting. “It seems inevitable that it will eventually destroy my save with a bug that’s been around since Oblivion.” Ominous.

No less ominous is Tuffcub playing Directive 8020 for our review next week. He also played Gears of War: Reloaded as he’d never played one before, and reckons it’s a “pile of pants”… so he’s returned to playing a game where shooting actually feels good, running around in Destiny 2 and just having fun, even if he’s no longer got a clue what’s going on in the story.

Steve has as always, been sampling indie games, which are summed up as “Midnight Saturn (cool point n click), Duck Side of the Moon (sweet cozy game), The Shore (not great), Dark Pals (fun short mascot horror), and some time with the Switch version of Quartet and three different horror adjacent titles that I can’t name quite yet. Oh, and I finished Replaced on Game Pass which was mostly great but had some serious pacing issues.”

Miguel’s had Saros on the go and some .Hack//Infection while emulating the PS2, and we’ll round things out with Dom, who’s been gushing about how good Mixtape is, and been doing rather well in Overwatch this season.

Now then, what have you played this week?



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/08/what-we-played-terratech-legion-saros-mixtape/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
